content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance                Administration            Executive-Legal          Enforcement            Statement of Economic Interest:
(916) 322-5660            322-5660                322-5601            322-6441            322-6444

May 11, 1981


MS. Teri Barthels
Personnel Services Section
Special Projects Unit
Department of Health Services
714 P Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re: Your Request for Determination

Dear Ms. Barthels:

We have reviewed the materials which you submitted
regarding the Regional Rabies Advisory Committee and the
Generally Handicapped Person's Program Advisory Committee.
We have also reviewed the reporting requirements required of
the members of these two advisory committees, which you understand stand from the Department's Conflict of Interest Code to be
Disclosure Categories 6 and 7.

After a review of these two committee's powers and
duties and the narrow disclosure of Categories 6 and 7, we
feel that such disclosures are appropriate. For the Rabies
Committee, the only likely disclosures will be under the
requirements of Category 7, and only the Veterinarian or
Livestock Industry representative are likely to have any
interests which must be disclosed. Disclosure under these
circumstances is not burdensome. For the Genetically handicapped Committee, disclosure will most likely occur, if at
all, under Category 6, if one of the members owns stock,
etc., in a business receiving grants, etc., from the Department.
Again, disclosure is narrow and not unduly burdensome.

These committee members were given an extension of the
April 1, 1981, filing deadline while we considered this
matter. Now that we have determined that filing is appropriate, they should file their Statements of Economic Interest
on or before June 1, 1981.

                                        - Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert B. Ledigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

REL:kp
cc: Robert Stern
    FPPC General Counsel

State of California                                    Department of Health Services

Memorandum

To    : Bob Leidigh                         Date  : April 13, 1981
       Legal Office
       Fair Political Practices Commission

Subject: Conflict of Interest
         Filing Requirements


From : Administration Division
       9/840            5-1154


       This memo is the Department of Health Services' formal request that F.P.P.C.
       determine whether the Regional Rabies Advisory Committees in Los Angeles,
       Fresno, Monterey, Berkeley, Eureka, and Sacramento, and the Genetically
       Handicapped Person's Program Advisory Committee have been appropriately
       required to file statements of economic interest under the Department's
       Conflict of Interest Code and confirms that the F.P.P.C. has suspended
       the filing deadline for the members of the committees until a determination
       is made, per our phone conversation of April 8, 1981.

       I am enclosing summary statements of the authority for establishment,
       composition, and purposes of the committees in question. The information
       was compiled in June 1977, but no major changes have occurred in the
       interim. It will be available to research and provide additional in-
       formation if necessary.


                                        Teri Barthels
                                        Personnel Services Section
                                        Special Projects Unit

cc: Pliney Young

    Arthur E. Jordan

    Bruce Pomer

    Richard Koppes


TB:ab
